data.title: B2gold targets 720,000 ounces of new annual production with Goose, Fekola, and Gramalote projects
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: B2Gold Corp. (BTG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Clive Johnson highlighted a strong start to 2025, with all mines performing well. He noted that issues at Fekola, which impacted 2024 performance, have been resolved. Production from Fekola Regional could add 180,000 ounces annually, while the Goose project is expected to contribute 300,000 ounces annually upon ramp-up. Combined, these projects contribute to a projected total of approximately 720,000 ounces of new annual production, including potential output from the Gramalote project in Colombia.

  • Johnson stated, “We have no interest in acquiring new development companies or projects, focusing instead on advancing our current growth profile.” He emphasized the near-term impact of Goose and Fekola projects and the feasibility study for Gramalote expected by mid-2025.

  • CFO Mike Cinnamond reported $0.09 adjusted earnings per share for Q1 2025, with basic earnings per share at $0.04. Operating cash flow before working capital adjustments was $244 million. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $330 million, with the revolving credit facility fully undrawn, providing significant financial flexibility.

  • Senior Vice President Bill Lytle noted significant progress at Goose, highlighting that construction activities are nearly complete, and the project is on track for first gold and ramp-up to commercial production by Q3 2025. He also mentioned record underground mining rates at Umwelt and strong performance across the company’s other operations in Mali, Masbate, and Otjikoto.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed the Goose project's timeline, with first gold production anticipated by Q2 2025 and ramp-up to commercial production in Q3 2025.

  • The Fekola Regional exploitation permit is expected within 30 to 60 days, with pre-stripping activities requiring three months before ore production can commence.

  • The feasibility study for the Gramalote project is on track for completion by mid-2025. If favorable, a development decision could position Gramalote for production later in the decade.

  • Management emphasized maintaining robust exploration programs, with $64 million allocated for corporate exploration in 2025, including $32 million focused on Back River.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted earnings per share were $0.09, supported by a strong average gold sales price. Basic earnings per share were $0.04, impacted by non-cash adjustments related to the Goose acquisition and zero-cost collars.

  • Operating cash flow before working capital adjustments reached $244 million. Capital expenditures at Goose were CAD 136 million for Q1 2025, aligned with the CAD 1.54 billion total budget.

  • The company reported a strong balance sheet with $330 million in cash and cash equivalents. The revolving credit facility remains fully undrawn, providing $800 million in available liquidity.

Q&A

  • Francesco Costanzo, Scotiabank: Asked about critical path items for Goose. Bill Lytle responded that commissioning of the powerhouse and mill circuits are key milestones, with first gold expected by the end of Q2 2025.

  • Anita Soni, CIBC World Markets: Inquired about the Gramalote feasibility study parameters and potential gold price assumptions. CFO Mike Cinnamond indicated consensus prices of $2,300 to $2,400 per ounce as a base for scenarios.

  • Carey Macrury, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about stockpiles for Goose ahead of mill startup. Lytle confirmed significant stockpile preparation, with expectations of running high-grade ore through the mill during commissioning.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in the progress of key projects and were generally positive about management’s detailed updates on Goose and Fekola.

  • Management conveyed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing achievements and future growth catalysts. CEO Clive Johnson reassured stakeholders about the company’s strategic focus and financial position.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed a more optimistic tone, reflecting progress on operational challenges and key projects.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize the resolution of past operational issues and anticipation of growth from Goose and Fekola.

  • Management’s tone was more confident, highlighting resolved challenges at Fekola and the successful advancement of Goose.

  • Analysts’ focus remained on project timelines and capital allocation, consistent with the previous quarter but with increased optimism about execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include the timely receipt of permits for Fekola Regional and the completion of Goose ramp-up activities. Management has outlined specific strategies to address these, including close collaboration with the Malian government.

  • Analysts raised concerns about capital spending and underground mining rates at Goose. Management assured that adjustments to equipment and personnel have mitigated these issues.

Final Takeaway

B2Gold’s Q1 2025 earnings call showcased significant progress across its key projects, notably the Goose and Fekola initiatives, positioning the company for substantial production growth. With first gold at Goose expected by Q2 2025 and the potential addition of 180,000 annualized ounces from Fekola Regional, the company is set to achieve a robust growth profile. A strong financial position and focused strategic execution further support B2Gold’s outlook for the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Coinbase Q1 profit falls short as crypto volatility dents trading volume; delivers soft Q2 guidance
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) stock dipped 3.2% in Thursday after-hours trading after the crypto exchange posted soft guidance for Q2 2025 and weaker-than-expected Q1 results, as crypto-market volatility during the three-month period drove a decline in trading volumes on the platform.

For Q2, the company expects subscription and services revenue of $600M-$680M (midpoint $640M), missing the $704.3M Visible Alpha estimate; technology & development + general & administrative expenses of $700M-$750M (midpoint $725M); and sales and marketing expenses of $215M-$315M (midpoint $265M). It also expects Q2 transaction expenses as a percentage of net revenue to be in the mid-teens.

Turning to Q1 2024 results, GAAP adjusted EPS of $1.94, trailing the $1.98 average analyst estimate, dropped from $3.37 in the prior quarter and $2.53 a year before.

Q1 total revenue of $2.03B, also weaker than the $2.09B consensus, fell from $2.27B in Q4 2024, but advanced from $1.64B in the year-ago period.

Transaction revenue was $1.26B in Q1, down from $1.56B in Q4 and up from $1.08B in 2024's Q1.

Subscription and services revenue rose to $698.1M in Q1 (vs. $694.9M Visible Alpha consensus) from $641.1M in Q4 and $510.9M in Q1 2024.

Total operating expenses mounted to $2.33B vs. $1.34B in Q4 and $877.1M in Q1 2024. 

Trading volume totaled $393B in Q1 (vs. $398.9B Visible Alpha estimate), compared with $439M in Q4 and $312M in last year's Q1.

Q1 adjusted EBITDA slid to $930M (vs. $939M Visible Alpha estimate) from $1.29B in the earlier quarter and $1.01B in Q1 2024. 

Conference call at 5:30 p.m. ET.

data.title: Federal REIT FFO of $1.70 beats by $0.01, revenue of $309.15M beats by $1.61M
data.description:
• Federal REIT press release (NYSE:FRT): Q1 FFO of $1.70 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $309.15M (+6.1% Y/Y) beats by $1.61M.
  • Shares +2.72%.
  • Generated comparable property operating income (POI) growth of 2.8%, excluding lease termination fees and prior period rents collected.
  • Reported comparable portfolio occupancy of 93.6% and a leased rate of 95.9% at quarter end, representing a change of:
    • +180 basis points of occupancy and +160 basis points of leased rate year-over-year
    • -10 basis points of occupancy and -20 basis points of leased rate quarter-over-quarter
  • Continued strong small shop leased rate, ending the quarter at 93.5% leased representing an increase of +210 basis points year-over-year.
  • Extended our $600 million unsecured term loan maturity date to March 2028, plus 2 one-year extension options, and increased the potential size to $750 million. We ended the quarter with nearly $1.5 billion of total liquidity.
  • Subsequent to quarter end, announced a new common share repurchase program, under which we may purchase up to $300 million of our outstanding common shares.
  • Closed on our previously announced acquisition of Del Monte Shopping Center in Monterey, California.
  • Maintained 2025 earnings per diluted share guidance of $3.00 to $3.12 and raised 2025 FFO per share guidance to $7.11 to $7.23, which represents 6% growth at the midpoint year-over-year.
  • FY FFO consensus of $7.12

data.title: EPR Properties increases 2025 earnings guidance amid portfolio expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EPR Properties (EPR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Greg Silvers highlighted a 4.7% increase in top-line revenue and a 5.3% year-over-year growth in FFO per share for Q1 2025. He announced an upward revision of 2025 earnings guidance and noted progress in diversifying the portfolio through investments in experiential assets, such as a construction-themed attraction and a private golf club.
  • Silvers reported continued focus on reducing investments in theaters and education properties, noting the sale of several assets for $70.8 million in net proceeds and a gain of $9.4 million. The capital is being redeployed into experiential properties.
  • CFO Mark Peterson detailed financial performance, including FFO adjusted for Q1 at $1.19 per share, a 5% increase from the prior year. AFFO per share also grew by 8% year-over-year.
  • Peterson announced an increase in 2025 FFO guidance to $5.00-$5.16 per share, citing improved financial performance and additional percentage rents and participating income.

Outlook

  • Management maintained their investment spending guidance for 2025 at $200 million to $300 million, with approximately $87 million allocated for experiential development and redevelopment projects this year.
  • Disposition guidance for 2025 was increased to $80 million to $120 million, reflecting the recent sales momentum.
  • Expected percentage rents and participating interest income were raised to a range of $21.5 million to $25.5 million, primarily due to $2.9 million in prior period income recognized in Q1 and higher current-year expectations.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q1 2025 was reported at $175 million, up from $167.2 million in the prior year. Rental revenue increased by $4.1 million, driven by new investments and higher percentage rents.
  • Mortgage and other financing income rose by $4.1 million, attributed to additional mortgage note investments and participating interest income related to ski properties.
  • Interest expense increased by $1.4 million due to higher borrowings under the unsecured revolving credit facility.
  • The company reported no remaining vacant early childhood education centers and stated that ongoing dispositions are expected to streamline the portfolio further.

Q&A

  • Anthony Paolone, JPMorgan, inquired about the golf investment’s structure and operator background. CEO Silvers explained that it involves a private club tied to homeownership and highlighted the growing scarcity and opportunity in this sector.
  • Bennett Rose, Citi, questioned the robustness of the buyer market for disposals. Silvers mentioned a strong buyer interest, particularly in education assets, with competitive bidding processes.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential impacts from tariffs on development projects. Silvers stated that current projects are secured with pricing but acknowledged it as a discussion point for future developments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed interest in the company’s strategic shifts, particularly in experiential investments and asset disposals. Questions were constructive but touched on potential risks, such as tariffs and operational expenses.
  • Management remained confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing portfolio resilience and growth potential. During Q&A, the tone was slightly cautious when discussing specific challenges, such as operational costs and insurance pressures.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated greater confidence in their ability to execute on guidance and expand the portfolio.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter reflected a stronger focus on new experiential investments, such as the introduction of unique asset types like a construction-themed attraction and a private golf club. These were not emphasized in Q4 2024.
  • Disposition guidance increased significantly from the prior quarter, showcasing accelerated progress in portfolio rebalancing.
  • Financial guidance was revised upwards, while last quarter’s guidance remained more conservative.
  • Management’s tone showed heightened optimism about the box office recovery and its impact on percentage rents.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing cost pressures, particularly related to insurance and operational expenses, as challenges for both the company and its tenants.
  • Analysts raised concerns about potential impacts from tariffs on future development and the execution of build-to-suit projects.
  • The company’s reliance on consistent box office recovery and experiential spending was highlighted as a key factor for maintaining rent coverage.

Final Takeaway

EPR Properties demonstrated strong Q1 2025 results, reflecting growth in revenue and earnings, while raising its 2025 earnings guidance. The company made significant progress in diversifying its portfolio and reducing exposure to theaters and education assets. Management’s confidence in experiential investments, combined with a strengthened financial outlook and robust disposition activity, positions EPR Properties well for the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
